summary.text: DETR-style object detection can be organized by research route and technical mechanism: the route starts from query-based set prediction, then branches into convergence, multi-scale attention, 3D extension, and real-time deployment concerns.

axes.axis_rationale: The playbook workset is small, so the route axis keeps only the DETR-family route that is directly supported by source papers.

nodes.title: DETR-family set prediction detectors
nodes.definition: Object detection methods derived from DETR that use queries and set prediction instead of anchor/NMS-centered pipelines.
nodes.core_problem: Reduce hand-designed detection pipeline components while preserving detection accuracy and deployability.
nodes.mechanism: Transformer object queries, bipartite matching, sparse attention, denoising, and deployment-oriented architecture choices.
strengths: Clear route for query and matching improvements
limitations: The bounded playbook omits many DETR variants
nodes.maturity: established research family with active efficiency and deployment work

axes.axis_rationale: The mechanism axis separates set prediction, sparse attention, denoising, 3D queries, and real-time constraints.

nodes.title: Query-based set prediction
nodes.definition: A detector mechanism that predicts object sets through learned queries and matching.
nodes.core_problem: Replace proposal generation and post-processing with a direct prediction objective.
nodes.mechanism: Object queries interact with image features and are matched to targets with Hungarian matching.
limitations: Requires later work for fast convergence and multi-scale detail
nodes.maturity: foundational mechanism for the DETR family

summary.text: The bounded workset presents a compact timeline: original DETR defines the paradigm, Deformable DETR and DINO improve training and multi-scale behavior, DETR3D extends the idea to 3D detection, and RF-DETR represents recent real-time deployment pressure.

events.label: DETR introduces set prediction detection
events.description: DETR formulates object detection as direct set prediction with object queries and Hungarian matching.

events.label: Convergence and query training improvements
events.description: Deformable DETR and DINO address convergence, attention sparsity, and query/anchor training stability.

events.label: 3D extension and real-time deployment pressure
events.description: DETR3D and RF-DETR show the paradigm moving beyond 2D COCO-style detection into 3D and deployment-oriented settings.

claims.text: DETR reframes object detection as set prediction and reduces reliance on anchor/NMS style hand-designed components.
claims.analysis: The original DETR paper supplies the paradigm-defining evidence through object queries and Hungarian matching.
claims.scope: Bounded to the DETR-family workset in this playbook.

claims.text: Representative follow-up work focuses on convergence, multi-scale attention, and query/anchor training stability.
claims.analysis: Deformable DETR and DINO provide direct evidence for this optimization route.
claims.scope: Bounded to the selected playbook papers.

improvement_dimension_summary.summary: The current workset supports convergence, multi-scale attention, query training, 3D extension, and real-time deployment as improvement dimensions.

improvement_dimensions.title: Convergence and multi-scale attention
improvement_dimensions.analysis: Deformable attention and DINO-style denoising address the slow convergence and small-object limitations of early DETR.

improvement_dimensions.title: 3D and real-time deployment extension
improvement_dimensions.analysis: DETR3D and RF-DETR show the query/set-prediction idea moving into 3D perception and real-time detector constraints.

debates.title: Whether end-to-end set prediction can meet real-time detector constraints
debates.current_judgment: The playbook evidence shows active movement toward real-time constraints, but the bounded workset is insufficient for a field-wide conclusion.

future_directions.title: Complete DETR-family coverage
future_directions.current_limitation: The bounded playbook workset omits many DETR variants and review papers.
future_directions.future_direction: Collect RT-DETR, DN-DETR, Conditional DETR, and survey papers before treating the synthesis as complete.
future_directions.rationale: A full topic synthesis should not infer the whole DETR-family landscape from five representative papers.

review_outline.topic_importance: DETR-style object detection is a useful topic because it illustrates how set prediction reshapes detector design and follow-up optimization.

writing_strategies.title: Route-first DETR review
writing_strategies.review_thesis: Use original DETR as the route definition, then organize follow-up work by convergence, multi-scale attention, extension, and deployment pressure.
writing_strategies.writing_strategy: Start with the modeling shift, then map each representative paper to a specific bottleneck or extension.
writing_strategies.best_for: A compact related-work section about DETR-family detection.
writing_strategies.risks: The bounded playbook does not cover the full DETR-family literature.
